title: IIoT Industrial Automation
description: Learn to secure and operate Industrial Internet of Things environments with SCADA systems, PLCs, and MES integration.
prerequisites: Familiarity with industrial control systems concepts
learningObjectives: Integrate data historian with MES systems

levels.title: SCADA Fundamentals
levels.description: Learn the basics of SCADA systems and HMI operation

tasks.title: Connect to SCADA/HMI Workstation
tasks.description: Access the SCADA/HMI workstation via VNC to understand the control interface
tasks.hint: Use the VNC console to connect to the SCADA workstation. Look for the main control panel application.

tasks.title: Monitor System Status
tasks.description: Check the current status of all connected devices on the SCADA dashboard
tasks.hint: Navigate to the device status tab. You should see PLC, OPC-UA server, and data historian status indicators.

tasks.title: Acknowledge System Alerts
tasks.description: Review and acknowledge any pending alerts in the SCADA system
tasks.hint: Check the alerts panel. Some devices may show warning states that need acknowledgment.

tasks.title: Configure Alarm Thresholds
tasks.description: Set appropriate alarm thresholds for temperature monitoring
tasks.hint: Find the configuration section for alarm settings. Temperature thresholds should be configurable between 60-80 degrees.

levels.title: PLC & OPC-UA Configuration
levels.description: Configure PLC simulators and establish secure OPC-UA communication

tasks.title: Access PLC Simulator
tasks.description: Connect to the PLC simulator and examine the running program
tasks.hint: The PLC simulator runs on port 5000. Access it via web interface or terminal to view ladder logic.

tasks.title: Modify PLC Tags
tasks.description: Update the PLC tag values for tank level and valve positions
tasks.hint: Use the PLC simulator interface to modify tags. Tank level should be 0-100%, valve positions are 0 (closed) or 1 (open).

tasks.title: Configure OPC-UA Server
tasks.description: Set up the OPC-UA server to expose PLC data securely
tasks.hint: The OPC-UA server configuration is in /etc/opcua/. Update the endpoint URL and enable security policies.

tasks.title: Establish OPC-UA Connection
tasks.description: Verify that SCADA can connect to OPC-UA server and read PLC data
tasks.hint: Use the SCADA client to browse OPC-UA nodes. You should see all PLC tags available.

levels.title: Data Historian & MES Integration
levels.description: Configure data historian collection and MES system integration

tasks.title: Configure Data Historian
tasks.description: Set up the data historian to collect process data at appropriate intervals
tasks.hint: Edit the historian configuration in /etc/historian/. Set collection interval to 5 seconds for key tags.

tasks.title: Create Production Records
tasks.description: Create a production batch record in the MES system
tasks.hint: Access the MES web interface. Navigate to production records and create a new batch with product ID and quantity.

tasks.title: Link Historian to MES
tasks.description: Associate collected data with the production batch in MES
tasks.hint: In the MES system, link the historian data tags to the batch record created in the previous task.

tasks.title: Generate Production Report
tasks.description: Generate a production report combining MES and historian data
tasks.hint: Use the reporting feature in MES to create a PDF report showing batch performance metrics.

tasks.title: Implement Security Hardening
tasks.description: Apply security best practices to the IIoT environment
tasks.hint: Review and apply: firewall rules, OPC-UA security policies, disable unused services, update credentials.
